Recent papers

Recent papers on the Riemann hypothesis, surfaced by the prominence of their authors in this directory.

Showing 15 papers, ranked by the Top 100 standing of their authors then by recency. Authors who appear in the Top 100 are shown in bold.

#TitleAuthorsPublishedBest rank
1Short mollifiers of the Riemann zeta-functionJ. Brian Conrey, David W. Farmer, Chung-Hang Kwan, Yongxiao Lin, Caroline L. Turnage-Butterbaugh2025-08#1
2Binomial coefficients with divisors avoiding an intervalHung M. Bui, Kyle Pratt, Alexandru Zaharescu2026-05#2
3Simultaneous non-vanishing of Dirichlet L-functionsHung M. Bui, Alexandra Florea, Micah B. Milinovich2026-04#2
4Amplified Fourth Moment of the Riemann Zeta-Function and ApplicationsHung M. Bui, Richard R. Hall, Martin Subira Jorge2025-11#2
5Simultaneous nonvanishing of Dirichlet $L$-functions in Galois orbitsHung M. Bui, Alexandra Florea, Hieu T. Ngo2025-07#2
6High moments of random multiplicative functions twisted by Fourier coefficients of modular formsPeng Gao, Liangyi Zhao2026-06#4
7Shifted moments of modular $L$-functions to a fixed levelPeng Gao, Liangyi Zhao2026-02#4
8First Moment of Quadratic Hecke $L$-Functions with Lower Order TermPeng Gao, Liangyi Zhao2025-12#4
9Lower Bounds on High Moments of Twisted Fourier coefficients of Modular FormsPeng Gao, Liangyi Zhao2025-11#4
10The mollified fourth moment of Dirichlet $L$-functionsPeng Gao, Xiaosheng Wu, Liangyi Zhao2025-09#4
11Shifted moments of cubic and quartic Dirichlet $L$-functionsPeng Gao, Liangyi Zhao2025-08#4
12Twisted fourth moment of Dirichlet $L$-functions to a fixed modulusPeng Gao, Liangyi Zhao2025-07#4
13Zeta Zeros in a Narrow Vertical BoxDaniel A. Goldston, Ade Irma Suriajaya2026-03#5
14Zeta Zeros on the Critical LineDaniel A. Goldston, Ade Irma Suriajaya2025-11#5
15The Alternative Hypothesis for Zeros of the Riemann Zeta-FunctionSiegfred Alan C. Baluyot, Daniel Alan Goldston, Ade Irma Suriajaya, Caroline L. Turnage-Butterbaugh2025-08#5

Most cited (all time)

The ten most-cited Mathematics works on the Riemann hypothesis, by citation count in OpenAlex. Results are filtered to the Mathematics field (OpenAlex field 26) and ranked by total citations.

The Top 100 ranking is designed to capture prominent, currently active researchers. If an author in this table is not linked to a profile, it is usually because they are deceased or less active than the researchers in the ranking, not because they are less significant to the field.

#TitleAuthorsYearCitations
1The Theory of the Riemann Zeta-FunctionE. C. Titchmarsh; D. R. Heath‐Brown19873,273
2The Riemann Zeta Function DOIAnatolij A. Karatsuba; Melvyn B. Nathanson1992904
3Contributions to the theory of the riemann zeta-function and the theory of the distribution of primes DOIG. H. Hardy; J. E. Littlewood1916454
4Limit Theorems for the Riemann Zeta-Function DOIAntanas Laurinčikas1996376
5Theorem on the “Universality” of the Riemann Zeta-function DOIСергей Воронин1975344
6Trace formula in noncommutative geometry and the zeros of the Riemann zeta function DOIAlain Connes1999342
7Distribution functions and the Riemann zeta function DOIBørge Jessen; Aurel Wintner1935331
8Mean-Value Theorems in the Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function DOIA. E. Ingham1928277
9Decoupling, exponential sums and the Riemann zeta function DOIJean Bourgain2016264
10Spectral Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function DOIYoichi Motohashi1997262

Maintained by Steve Hubbard (admin@wwirh.org). Data sources: arXiv, OpenAlex, zbMATH Open, Mathematics Genealogy Project.
